properties.hook: One of the largest and finest Baroque old towns in Northern Europe, with a bohemian breakaway "republic" of artists across the river that has its own tongue-in-cheek constitution.

properties.name: Andasibe & the Eastern Rainforests
properties.hook: An accessible eastern rainforest where the eerie, whale-like song of the indri, the largest living lemur, echoes through the trees at dawn, and a guided walk brings you face to face with creatures found only on this island.

properties.name: The Baobabs & Tsingy
properties.hook: Western Madagascar's twin wonders: a dirt road lined with giant, ancient baobab trees glowing at sunset, and, further north, a forest of razor-sharp limestone pinnacles you cross on ladders and rope bridges.

properties.name: Nosy Be & the North
properties.hook: Madagascar's main beach island, a laid-back tropical escape of white sand, warm turquoise water, and the scent of ylang-ylang, with more islands, reefs, and lemur-filled reserves within easy reach.

properties.name: Lake Malawi
properties.hook: A vast, clear freshwater lake, the "lake of stars," so full of colourful fish found nowhere else that snorkelling in it feels like drifting through a tropical aquarium, ringed by beaches and sleepy fishing villages.

properties.name: Liwonde National Park
properties.hook: Malawi's finest wildlife park, a conservation success story along a broad river where, thanks to a dramatic rewilding, elephants, lions, and rhinos have returned to a landscape once emptied by poaching.

properties.name: Mount Mulanje
properties.hook: A colossal granite massif rising abruptly out of the tea plantations of southern Malawi, its sheer walls and plateaus of grassland and forest towering thousands of metres above the plain, laced with trekking routes between mountain huts.

properties.name: George Town, Penang
properties.hook: A former British trading port on an island where Malay, Chinese, Indian, and colonial worlds fused into a distinct culture, now a walkable old town of shophouses, clan temples, street art, and some of the best street food in Asia.

properties.name: Borneo: Kinabalu & Sabah
properties.hook: The Malaysian slice of Borneo, where one of Southeast Asia's highest peaks rises straight from the jungle, orphaned orangutans swing at a famous sanctuary, and a tiny island offshore is one of the planet's great dive sites.

properties.name: Taman Negara
properties.hook: One of the oldest tropical rainforests on earth, said to have grown undisturbed for well over a hundred million years, where you trek under giant trees, walk a swaying canopy bridge, and sleep to a wall of jungle sound.

properties.name: Djenné
properties.hook: The largest mud-brick building in the world, a magnificent, spiky-walled Great Mosque that is re-plastered by the whole town each year in a communal festival, at the heart of an ancient Sahel trading city.

properties.name: The Dogon Country
properties.hook: A dramatic sandstone escarpment where the Dogon people built villages into the cliffs and terraces, keeping one of Africa's most intricate cosmologies, mythologies, and mask traditions, long a legendary trekking destination now shadowed by conflict.

properties.name: Timbuktu
properties.hook: The name that stands, in many languages, for the most remote place on earth, once a fabled Saharan city of gold, salt, and scholarship, home to great mud mosques and libraries of hundreds of thousands of ancient manuscripts.

properties.name: Chinguetti & the Adrar
properties.hook: A near-abandoned medieval caravan town half-buried in the encroaching Sahara, where families still guard crumbling libraries of ancient hand-written manuscripts, once a great centre of learning on the road to Mecca.

properties.name: The Iron Ore Train
properties.hook: One of the longest and heaviest trains on earth, a two-and-a-half-kilometre snake of open ore wagons crossing the Sahara, which the truly intrepid ride for free by climbing aboard and lying atop the iron ore.

properties.name: The Richat Structure (Eye of the Sahara)
properties.hook: A vast geological bullseye in the Sahara, concentric rings of rock nearly forty kilometres across, so perfectly formed that astronauts use it as a landmark from orbit and it long baffled the people who lived beside it.

properties.name: Chamarel & the Interior
properties.hook: The green, mountainous heart of Mauritius, where volcanic soil forms a hillside of surreal rainbow-striped dunes, a high waterfall drops into a gorge, and the island's last native forest shelters birds saved from the brink.

properties.name: Le Morne & the Lagoons
properties.hook: A dramatic basalt mountain plunging into a turquoise lagoon at the island's tip, its beauty shadowed by a poignant history as a refuge for escaped slaves, and a base for some of the Indian Ocean's best beaches and water sports.

properties.name: Rodrigues
properties.hook: Mauritius's small, far-flung sister island, a slow, unspoiled world of a huge shallow lagoon, hilly Creole villages, and empty beaches, the way the main island was decades ago.

properties.name: Baja & the Sea of Cortez
properties.hook: A long, wild desert peninsula between two seas, where cardón cacti march to the shore and the Sea of Cortez, so teeming with life that Cousteau called it "the aquarium of the world," fills with whales, whale sharks, and swirling schools of fish.

properties.name: Chichén Itzá & the Yucatán
properties.hook: The most famous Maya city, crowned by a perfect stepped pyramid where, twice a year, the sun casts a shadow-serpent slithering down the staircase, at the heart of a region of jungle-shrouded ruins and swimmable sacred sinkholes.

properties.name: Copper Canyon
properties.hook: A network of vast canyons deeper and larger overall than the Grand Canyon, crossed by one of the world's great railway journeys, in a rugged sierra that is the homeland of the Rarámuri, famed as some of the finest long-distance runners on Earth.

properties.name: Guanajuato & the Bajío
properties.hook: A jewel-box colonial city that silver wealth built, its rainbow houses spilling up steep ravines, its traffic hidden in tunnels beneath, and its lanes so narrow that lovers can kiss across one balcony to another.

properties.name: Mexico City & Teotihuacán
properties.hook: One of the world's great capitals, built on the ruins of the Aztec island city of Tenochtitlán, layering ancient temples, colonial grandeur, world-class museums, and some of the best food anywhere, near the vast pyramids of a civilisation that vanished before the Aztecs arrived.

properties.name: The Monarch Butterfly Reserve
properties.hook: Every winter, hundreds of millions of monarch butterflies complete one of nature's most astonishing migrations, flying thousands of kilometres from Canada and the US to cluster in these few Mexican mountain forests, weighing down the fir branches and filling the air like drifting orange snow.

properties.name: Oaxaca
properties.hook: Mexico's culinary and cultural heart, a graceful colonial city where the food is a religion, mezcal flows, indigenous traditions run deep, and a hilltop of ancient Zapotec ruins overlooks it all.

properties.name: The Mileștii Mici Wine Cellars
properties.hook: An underground wine cellar so vast you tour it by car, more than a hundred and fifty kilometres of tunnels lined with millions of bottles, holding what is recognised as the largest wine collection in the world.

properties.name: Orheiul Vechi
properties.hook: A cave monastery carved into a limestone cliff above a looping river gorge in the Moldovan countryside, still used by monks, with a small stone cross marking the entrance on the clifftop.

properties.name: Transnistria
properties.hook: A sliver of land along the Dniester that broke away from Moldova in the early 1990s and declared itself a country nobody recognises, with its own flag, currency, and border posts, and a Soviet aesthetic it never let go of.

properties.name: The Altai Eagle Hunters
properties.hook: In Mongolia's far western mountains, ethnic Kazakh herders still hunt from horseback with trained golden eagles, a bond between human, bird, and landscape passed down for centuries.

properties.name: The Gobi Desert
properties.hook: A vast cold desert of gravel plains, singing sand dunes, and red cliffs where the first dinosaur eggs were found, crossed by nomads and their camels and emptier than almost anywhere on earth.

properties.name: Karakorum & the Orkhon Valley
properties.hook: The grassy valley that was the cradle of steppe empires and the site of Genghis Khan's capital, where a walled monastery built from the ruins of that lost city stands amid endless rolling grassland.

properties.name: The Bay of Kotor
properties.hook: A deep, twisting bay that looks like a fjord but is really a drowned river canyon, walled in by grey mountains, with a medieval Venetian town at the far end under a fortress wall that climbs the cliff.

properties.name: Durmitor & the Tara Canyon
properties.hook: A high massif of jagged peaks and glacial lakes above one of the deepest river canyons on earth, where the Tara has cut more than a kilometre down through the mountains.

properties.name: Ostrog Monastery
properties.hook: A white Orthodox monastery built directly into a vertical cliff face high above a valley, looking as though it has been glued to the rock, and one of the most visited pilgrimage sites in the Balkans.

properties.name: Aït Benhaddou & the Desert Road
properties.hook: A fortified village of red-earth towers rising in tiers above a river on the old caravan road to the Sahara, so cinematically perfect it has stood in for ancient cities in countless films.

properties.name: Chefchaouen
properties.hook: A small mountain town in the Rif whose old medina is painted almost entirely in shades of blue, lane after lane of cobalt and powder-blue walls, steps, and doorways climbing the hillside.

properties.name: Fès
properties.hook: The best-preserved medieval city in the Arab world, a vast walled medina of thousands of tangled car-free lanes where donkeys still carry the loads, artisans work as they have for centuries, and dyers stand waist-deep in vats of colour.

properties.name: The High Atlas & Toubkal
properties.hook: A wall of high mountains just south of Marrakech, home to Berber villages of flat-roofed houses on terraced slopes and to the highest peak in North Africa, walkable from a trailhead an hour from the city.

properties.name: Marrakech
properties.hook: The great red city at the foot of the Atlas, where a huge open square erupts each night into a carnival of food stalls, musicians, and storytellers, and a labyrinth of souks and hidden courtyard palaces waits behind rose-coloured walls.

properties.name: Rabat
properties.hook: Morocco's calm, walkable capital on the Atlantic, where a blue-and-white cliff-top kasbah, an unfinished medieval minaret, and a Roman-Islamic ruin overgrown with storks sit above a mellow modern city.

properties.name: Volubilis & Meknès
properties.hook: The best-preserved Roman ruins in Morocco, a whole provincial city of arches, columns, and intact floor mosaics standing among the wheat fields, beside a grand but overlooked former imperial capital.

properties.name: The Bazaruto Archipelago
properties.hook: A cluster of Indian Ocean islands ringed by turquoise lagoons and towering sand dunes, where traditional dhows sail warm clear water that shelters coral reefs, whales, and one of Africa's last populations of dugongs.

properties.name: Ilha de Moçambique & the Quirimbas
properties.hook: A tiny coral island that was once the capital of Portuguese East Africa, its faded stone town of churches, forts, and mansions crumbling gracefully by the Indian Ocean, gateway to a string of pristine tropical islands.

properties.name: Maputo
properties.hook: A warm, faded, thoroughly lived-in capital on the Indian Ocean, where crumbling Portuguese-colonial and Art Deco buildings, jacaranda-lined avenues, fiery peri-peri seafood, and a strong music scene mingle into something distinctly Mozambican.

properties.name: Bagan
properties.hook: A dusty plain beside the Irrawaddy studded with thousands of brick temples and pagodas built by the kings of a medieval empire, an ancient skyline best seen from a temple terrace at dawn as mist and balloons drift over the spires.

properties.name: Inle Lake
properties.hook: A calm highland lake where fishermen row standing on one leg, wrapping the other around the oar, and whole villages of stilt houses and floating vegetable gardens sit out on the water.

properties.name: Yangon
properties.hook: Myanmar's largest city, a humid sprawl of mouldering colonial mansions and busy tea shops gathered around the vast golden dome of the Shwedagon Pagoda, the country's most sacred site.

properties.name: Etosha National Park
properties.hook: A vast, shimmering salt pan so large it dominates the landscape, ringed by waterholes where, in the dry season, the wildlife comes to you, elephants, rhinos, lions, and giraffes gathering at the water against a stark white backdrop.

properties.name: The Skeleton Coast & Swakopmund
properties.hook: A bleak, fog-shrouded shore where the great dunes of the Namib run straight into the cold Atlantic, littered with the rusting hulks of shipwrecks and the bones of whales, with an incongruous little German-colonial resort town in the middle of it.

properties.name: Sossusvlei
properties.hook: A landscape of towering rust-red sand dunes, among the highest on earth, marching across the ancient Namib desert, and, hidden among them, a white clay pan studded with the black skeletons of long-dead trees.

properties.name: Annapurna & Pokhara
properties.hook: The gateway to some of the world's greatest and most accessible mountain trekking, where a relaxed lakeside town sits beneath a wall of Himalayan giants and trails lead for days or weeks into their heart.

properties.name: Everest & the Khumbu
properties.hook: The trek to the foot of the highest mountain on earth, through the homeland of the Sherpa, past monasteries and prayer flags to a base camp under an icefall, no climbing required.

properties.name: The Kathmandu Valley
properties.hook: A single valley holding three former royal cities and some of the holiest sites in Hinduism and Buddhism, a dense concentration of pagoda temples, palace squares, and pilgrim life, much of it rebuilt after a major earthquake.

properties.name: Amsterdam's Canal Ring
properties.hook: A city built as a set of concentric canal rings four centuries ago, still lined with narrow leaning merchant houses, best seen from a bike or the quiet back canals rather than the packed centre.

properties.name: De Hoge Veluwe & the Kröller-Müller
properties.hook: A national park of heath and pine you cross on free white bikes left out for anyone, with one of the world's great Van Gogh collections sitting in a museum in the middle of the woods.

properties.name: The Wadden Sea & Mudflat Walking
properties.hook: The largest unbroken system of tidal mudflats on Earth, where at low water you can walk for hours across the sea floor from the mainland to an offshore island, with a guide who knows which channels will drown you.

properties.name: The Aïr & Ténéré
properties.hook: One of the Sahara's most sublime landscapes: a range of dark volcanic mountains rising from a golden ocean of dunes, home to the Tuareg, ancient rock art, and a lone acacia that was once the most isolated tree on earth.

properties.name: The Last Giraffes of Kouré
properties.hook: The last wild giraffes in all of West Africa, a small, recovering herd of a pale, distinctive subspecies that roams freely among the villages and fields near Niger's capital, saved from the brink of extinction.

properties.name: Kano
properties.hook: One of the great cities of the Sahel, an ancient Hausa trading hub whose old town of earthen walls, vast markets, and centuries-old indigo dye pits offers a completely different Nigeria from the coastal megacities.

properties.name: Lagos
properties.hook: One of Africa's biggest and most electric cities, a roaring megacity of some twenty million where fortunes are chased, Afrobeats blasts from every corner, and the sheer energy and hustle are the whole experience.

properties.name: The Osun-Osogbo Sacred Grove
properties.hook: A dense forest along a sacred river, dotted with shrines and fantastical sculptures to the Yoruba gods, one of the last great sacred groves of a living African religion, and the stage for a huge annual festival to the river goddess Osun.

properties.name: Lake Ohrid
properties.hook: One of the oldest and deepest lakes in Europe, ringed by mountains, with a mellow old town of Byzantine churches climbing above the water and a small red-roofed chapel perched on a cliff over the shore.

properties.name: Matka Canyon
properties.hook: A steep river canyon with an emerald reservoir at the bottom, cliffside medieval monasteries, and a boat ride to a cave said to be one of the deepest underwater caves in the world, all half an hour from the capital.

properties.name: Skopje
properties.hook: A capital of two clashing faces: a genuine Ottoman old bazaar on one bank, and on the other a government-built fantasy of giant statues, fake-classical façades, and warrior fountains that has to be seen to be believed.

properties.name: The Lofoten Islands
properties.hook: Granite peaks a thousand meters high rise straight out of the Arctic sea, and red fishermen's cabins on stilts cling to the water at their feet. This far north, the light does the rest.

properties.name: Dhofar & Salalah
properties.hook: The far south of Oman, where a summer monsoon does something no other Arabian desert sees, turning the hills lush green and misty, in the ancient land of frankincense at the edge of the world's greatest sand sea.

properties.name: Muscat & the Hajar Mountains
properties.hook: A refreshingly low-rise, white-washed capital on the Gulf of Oman, gateway to jagged mountains hiding deep canyons, palm-shaded villages, and old forts, in one of Arabia's most welcoming and traditional countries.

properties.name: The Wahiba Sands
properties.hook: A sea of tall red-and-gold dunes rolling to the horizon, where you sleep in a Bedouin desert camp under the stars, an easy taste of the great Arabian sands within reach of the coast.

properties.name: Lahore
properties.hook: The cultural heart of Pakistan, a Mughal capital of vast red-sandstone mosques and forts, a labyrinthine walled old city, and a food scene locals will tell you is the whole point of the place.

properties.name: Mohenjo-daro
properties.hook: One of the world's first cities, a Bronze Age metropolis on the Indus laid out on a grid with brick houses, public baths, and covered drains 4,500 years ago, built by a civilisation whose writing we still cannot read.

properties.name: Taxila & Gandhara
properties.hook: The ruins of an ancient centre of learning where Greek and Buddhist worlds fused, producing the serene Gandhara sculpture that gave the Buddha his first human face.

properties.name: Chachapoyas & Kuélap
properties.hook: Northern Peru's answer to Machu Picchu without the crowds: a massive stone citadel wrapped in cloud forest, built by the "warriors of the clouds" long before the Inca, near cliff tombs guarded by staring figures and one of the world's tallest waterfalls.

properties.name: Choquequirao
properties.hook: There is a second Machu Picchu, larger than the first, and to reach it you have to walk for two days into a canyon. Almost no one does.

properties.name: Cusco & the Sacred Valley
properties.hook: The ancient capital of the Inca empire, the "navel of the world," where Spanish colonial churches and mansions were built directly on top of masterful Inca stonework, gateway to a valley strewn with ruins and Machu Picchu beyond.

properties.name: Lake Titicaca
properties.hook: The highest navigable lake in the world, an immense sheet of deep blue water high in the Andes, where people live on floating islands woven entirely from reeds and keep ancient traditions on islands where time seems to have stopped.

properties.name: Lima
properties.hook: One of the world's great food cities, a sprawling Pacific capital where clifftop bohemian districts overlook the surf, colonial palaces line the old centre, and the ceviche and fine dining are reason enough to visit.

properties.name: The Nazca Lines
properties.hook: Enormous figures, a hummingbird, a monkey, a spider, and straight lines running for kilometres, etched into the desert floor two thousand years ago, so vast they only reveal themselves from the air, and so mysterious no one is certain why they were made.

properties.name: The Peruvian Amazon
properties.hook: Peru's vast, pristine slice of the Amazon, among the most biodiverse places on the planet, where you can drift the rivers past caimans and giant otters, walk beneath the canopy, and watch hundreds of macaws set a clay riverbank ablaze with colour.

properties.name: The Banaue Rice Terraces
properties.hook: Vast amphitheatres of rice terraces carved by hand into steep mountainsides by the Ifugao people, some said to be up to two thousand years old, still farmed and still watered by an ancient system nobody has improved on.

properties.name: Bohol & the Chocolate Hills
properties.hook: An island scattered with over a thousand oddly uniform, cone-shaped hills that dry to chocolate brown in summer, home to the tarsier, a wide-eyed primate small enough to sit in your palm.

properties.name: Palawan
properties.hook: A long, thin island of jungle-topped limestone cliffs and hidden lagoons, where you hop by boat between turquoise coves, and, further south, sunken WWII wrecks and one of the world's longest navigable underground rivers.

properties.name: Białowieża Forest
properties.hook: One of the last surviving fragments of the primeval lowland forest that once blanketed Europe, and the home of the European bison, brought back from the edge of extinction after the last wild ones were shot here.

properties.name: Kraków & Wieliczka
properties.hook: Poland's old royal capital, one of the few big Polish cities the war left standing, with a vast medieval square, a hilltop castle, and a nearby salt mine hollowed into underground chapels.

properties.name: Warsaw's Old Town
properties.hook: A picturesque medieval old town that is barely eighty years old, rebuilt brick by brick from paintings and rubble after the Nazis deliberately razed the entire city.

properties.name: Coimbra
properties.hook: Home to one of the oldest universities in the world, whose gilded Baroque library is guarded at night by a resident colony of bats that eat the insects threatening the books.

properties.name: The Douro Valley
properties.hook: The oldest demarcated wine region in the world, where the Douro river winds between hillsides terraced by hand into thousands of narrow steps, all of it for port.

properties.name: Évora
properties.hook: A walled town in the empty Alentejo plains with a Roman temple still standing in its centre, a chapel lined floor to ceiling with human bones, and a stone circle older than Stonehenge in the fields nearby.

properties.name: Lisbon
properties.hook: A city of seven hills above the Tagus, rebuilt on a grid after one of history's most devastating earthquakes, where wooden trams grind up alleys and mournful fado drifts out of Alfama's doorways.

properties.name: Porto
properties.hook: The hard-working city that gave Portugal its name and its most famous wine, tumbling down granite banks to the Douro in a jumble of tiled façades, iron bridges, and stacked riverfront houses.

properties.name: Sintra
properties.hook: A cool, wooded hill above Lisbon that Portuguese kings and rich romantics filled with fantasy palaces, including a lurid yellow-and-red one on the summit and a garden with an initiation well you spiral down into the earth.

properties.name: Doha
properties.hook: A Gulf capital that turned its oil and gas wealth toward culture, anchored by one of the world's finest museums of Islamic art in a sculptural building on its own island, across the bay from a futuristic skyline.

properties.name: Khor Al Adaid (the Inland Sea)
properties.hook: A place where the sea reaches deep inland to meet the desert, an arm of the Gulf lapping at the foot of tall crescent dunes, reachable only by driving across the sand.

properties.name: The Danube Delta
properties.hook: Where the Danube finally reaches the Black Sea and dissolves into one of the best-preserved wetlands in Europe, a maze of channels, floating reed islands, and one of the largest reed beds on earth, thick with pelicans.

properties.name: The Transfăgărășan
properties.hook: A dizzying mountain road built by a dictator's army across the highest wall of the Carpathians, hairpinning up to a glacial lake near the top and only open for a few snow-free months a year.

properties.name: Transylvania & the Saxon Towns
properties.hook: A green upland of medieval German towns and fortified farm-churches under the Carpathians, marketed hard on a Dracula connection that is mostly invented, and quietly wonderful for reasons that have nothing to do with vampires.

properties.name: Kigali
properties.hook: One of Africa's cleanest, safest, and most orderly capitals, spread over green hills, and home to a memorial that confronts the 1994 genocide with unflinching honesty, essential to understanding the country.

properties.name: Lake Kivu & Nyungwe Forest
properties.hook: A big, calm lake fringed with beaches and terraced hills, beside one of the oldest rainforests in Africa, where a swaying canopy walkway hangs high above the trees and chimpanzees call through the mist.

properties.name: Volcanoes National Park
properties.hook: The misty, volcano-cradled forest where Dian Fossey made mountain gorillas known to the world, and where trekking up the slopes of the Virunga volcanoes to sit with a gorilla family is Rwanda's signature experience.

properties.name: AlUla & Hegra
properties.hook: Saudi Arabia's answer to Petra: monumental tombs carved into desert sandstone outcrops by the same Nabataean people, standing almost alone in a dramatic oasis valley that until recently almost no outsider had seen.

properties.name: Diriyah & Riyadh
properties.hook: On the edge of the capital, the restored mud-brick town where the Saudi state was born, a warren of earthen palaces and walls beside a palm-filled valley, now the historic counterpoint to Riyadh's glass towers.

properties.name: The Empty Quarter (Rub' al Khali)
properties.hook: The largest continuous sand desert on earth, an ocean of dunes hundreds of metres high stretching to the horizon across the south of the Arabian Peninsula, one of the emptiest and most forbidding places on the planet.

properties.name: Dakar & Gorée Island
properties.hook: West Africa's most vibrant capital, thrumming with music and markets on the continent's westernmost point, with a short ferry to a beautiful, painful island that has become a memorial to the transatlantic slave trade.

properties.name: Saint-Louis
properties.hook: A crumbling, characterful old colonial town on a narrow island at the mouth of the Senegal River, once the capital of French West Africa, now faded, arty, and famous for its jazz, beside one of the world's great bird sanctuaries.

properties.name: The Sine-Saloum Delta
properties.hook: A vast maze of mangrove channels, tidal flats, and islands where two rivers meet the Atlantic, some of them built up over centuries from millions of discarded shells, alive with pelicans, flamingos, and pirogue-borne fishing life.

properties.name: Belgrade
properties.hook: A blunt, energetic capital where the Sava meets the Danube under an old fortress that has been fought over and destroyed dozens of times, now known as much for river-barge nightclubs as for its scarred history.

properties.name: Novi Sad & Fruška Gora
properties.hook: Serbia's easygoing second city, spread along the Danube beneath a huge star-shaped fortress that hosts one of Europe's big summer music festivals, backed by a ridge dotted with old monasteries.

properties.name: The Šargan Eight & Mokra Gora
properties.hook: A narrow-gauge heritage railway that loops through the mountains in a figure eight to climb a steep grade, paired with a hilltop wooden village a film director built for a movie and then kept.

properties.name: Freetown & the Peninsula Beaches
properties.hook: A hilly, hectic capital founded as a home for freed slaves, backed by forested mountains that tumble down to a string of white-sand beaches so beautiful they once starred in a chocolate-bar advert as the very image of paradise.

properties.name: Tiwai Island
properties.hook: A small forested island in a jungle river, one of the most biodiverse spots in West Africa for its size, home to a dozen kinds of primate and the elusive, rarely-seen pygmy hippopotamus.

properties.name: Bratislava
properties.hook: A small, easygoing capital on the Danube, overshadowed by nearby Vienna and all the better for it, with a compact old town, a blocky white castle, and bronze statues of eccentrics popping out of the pavement.

properties.name: The High Tatras
properties.hook: A compact wall of sharp granite peaks, the highest in the Carpathians, packed with glacial tarns and mountain huts, giving big-alpine scenery in a range you can cross on foot in a day or two.

properties.name: Spiš Castle & Levoča
properties.hook: One of the largest castle complexes in Central Europe, a vast sprawl of pale ruined walls on a bare hill above the Spiš plain, with a walled medieval town nearby holding the tallest Gothic wooden altar in the world.

properties.name: Lake Bled
properties.hook: The postcard of the Julian Alps: a small green lake with a church on a tiny island in the middle and a medieval castle on a cliff above, backed by snow peaks.

properties.name: Ljubljana & the Karst Caves
properties.hook: One of Europe's most relaxed small capitals, a car-free riverside centre of bridges guarded by dragons, within easy reach of the limestone country that gave the whole world the word "karst."

properties.name: Piran
properties.hook: Slovenia's short Adriatic coast squeezes in one gem: a small Venetian town packed onto a point, all terracotta roofs and a marble square, with medieval salt pans still worked by hand nearby.

properties.name: Cape Town & Table Mountain
properties.hook: One of the most beautifully sited cities on earth, wrapped around a flat-topped mountain that drops to the sea, where you can ride a cable car above the clouds in the morning and swim off a white beach in the afternoon.

properties.name: The Cape Winelands
properties.hook: A region of vineyards cradled by dramatic mountains just inland of Cape Town, dotted with gabled white Cape Dutch estates, some of the oldest in the Southern Hemisphere, pouring world-class wine over long lunches.

properties.name: The Drakensberg
properties.hook: South Africa's grandest mountains, a towering wall of basalt cliffs and green foothills rising to the roof of the country, hiding thousands of ancient San rock paintings in its caves and overhangs.

properties.name: The Garden Route
properties.hook: A famously scenic stretch of South Africa's southern coast, where indigenous forest, cliffs, lagoons, and beaches string together into one of the country's classic self-drive road trips.

properties.name: Johannesburg, Soweto & the Cradle of Humankind
properties.hook: South Africa's biggest, rawest city, where the story of apartheid and the struggle against it is told with unflinching power, and where, just outside town, caves have yielded some of the richest evidence of human origins on earth.

properties.name: Kruger National Park
properties.hook: One of Africa's great game reserves, the size of a small country, where you can drive yourself past lions, elephants, and rhinos on tarred roads, making a Big Five safari genuinely affordable and independent.

properties.name: Namaqualand & the Kalahari
properties.hook: A semi-desert that stays brown and empty most of the year, then, for a few weeks each spring, erupts into a carpet of orange and white wildflowers stretching to the horizon, in a corner of the country that also holds the red dunes of the Kalahari.

properties.name: Gyeongju
properties.hook: The capital of the ancient Silla kingdom for a thousand years, often called a museum without walls, where grassy royal burial mounds rise between the streets and a perfect stone Buddha watches the sunrise from a hilltop grotto.

properties.name: Jeju Island
properties.hook: A volcanic island south of the mainland built around Korea's highest mountain, ringed by black-rock coast, lava tubes, and craters, and home to the haenyeo, generations of women who free-dive for seafood without air tanks.

properties.name: Seoul & the DMZ
properties.hook: A hyper-modern megacity of neon, K-pop, and endless food, wrapped around granite mountains and old palaces, an hour from the most heavily fortified border on earth.

properties.name: The Alhambra
properties.hook: The last palace of Muslim Spain, a fortress on a hill above Granada whose inner rooms are carved into lace, held by the Nasrids until 1492 and nearly torn down for centuries after.

properties.name: The Mezquita of Córdoba
properties.hook: A vast former mosque whose interior is a hypnotic forest of red-and-white striped double arches, with an entire Christian cathedral built into the middle of it centuries later.

properties.name: The Picos de Europa
properties.hook: A wall of sheer grey limestone rising a few miles inland from the wet northern coast, close enough to the sea that sailors used it to know they were nearly home.

properties.name: Gaudí's Barcelona
properties.hook: A basilica begun in 1882 and still unfinished, its interior a forest of branching stone columns under coloured light, the strangest and most ambitious building of Antoni Gaudí's strange, ambitious city.

properties.name: Santiago de Compostela
properties.hook: The end of the Camino, where pilgrims have walked across Europe for a thousand years to reach one cathedral, and where a giant silver censer swings across the transept on a rope, trailing smoke.

properties.name: Toledo
properties.hook: A whole medieval city crammed onto a rock inside a loop of the Tagus, where for a few centuries Muslims, Jews, and Christians built side by side before the Inquisition ended it.

properties.name: Galle & the South Coast
properties.hook: A walled colonial port on the south coast, its ramparts enclosing a mix of Dutch, Portuguese, and Sri Lankan streets, backed by a coastline of beaches, blue whales, and fishermen perched on stilts in the surf.

properties.name: Kandy & the Hill Country
properties.hook: The last royal capital, wrapped around a lake and guarding a temple that holds a tooth of the Buddha, and the gateway to green tea highlands crossed by a slow, spectacular train.

properties.name: Sigiriya & the Cultural Triangle
properties.hook: A sheer rock rising two hundred metres from the jungle, its summit once crowned by a king's palace reached through the paws of a giant carved lion, at the centre of a landscape of ruined royal cities and cave temples.

properties.name: Jebel Barkal & Karima
properties.hook: A lone flat-topped mountain rising from the Nile-side desert, held sacred for thousands of years as a home of the god Amun, ringed by ancient temples and its own field of royal pyramids.

properties.name: The Pyramids of Meroë
properties.hook: More pyramids than all of Egypt, steeper, smaller, and standing almost alone in the rolling sands of the Sudanese desert, the royal tombs of the ancient Black African kingdom of Kush.

properties.name: Stockholm & the Archipelago
properties.hook: A capital built across fourteen islands where the lake meets the Baltic, opening straight out into an archipelago of some thousands more that locals scatter to by ferry all summer.

properties.name: Swedish Lapland
properties.hook: The Swedish Arctic, where the sun doesn't set for weeks in summer and doesn't rise for weeks in winter, home to the Sámi, the northern lights, and a hotel rebuilt out of river ice every year.

properties.name: Visby & Gotland
properties.hook: One of the best-preserved medieval walled towns in northern Europe, ringed by a nearly complete stone rampart, on a Baltic island of sheep, roses, and strange sea-stacks.

properties.name: The Aletsch Glacier
properties.hook: The largest glacier in the Alps, a twenty-kilometre river of ice curving between peaks, viewed from ridge-top villages reached only by cable car, and visibly retreating year by year.

properties.name: The Glacier Express & the Bernina Line
properties.hook: A day-long train billed as the slowest express in the world, crawling across a viaduct that curves into a cliff, over passes, and down to Italian-speaking valleys, on a line hand-built through mountains a century ago.

properties.name: Lauterbrunnen & the Jungfrau
properties.hook: A flat green valley walled in by sheer cliffs with seventy-two waterfalls spilling off the tops, and car-free villages perched on the rim above it. Tolkien is said to have modelled Rivendell on it.

properties.name: Lucerne & Lake Lucerne
properties.hook: A medieval town on a lake ringed by mountains, with a covered wooden footbridge from the fourteenth century and cog railways and cable cars up peaks that rise straight from the water.

properties.name: The Matterhorn & Zermatt
properties.hook: The most recognisable mountain in the Alps, a near-perfect rock pyramid standing alone above a car-free village, first climbed in a race that killed four men on the way down.

properties.name: The Rhine Falls
properties.hook: One of the most powerful waterfalls in Europe, where the whole Rhine pours over a wide ledge with a jagged rock standing in the middle of the torrent that you can climb by boat.

properties.name: The Fann Mountains
properties.hook: A compact range of sharp peaks and impossibly coloured turquoise lakes within reach of the capital, some of the finest and most accessible mountain trekking in Central Asia.

properties.name: The Pamir Highway
properties.hook: One of the world's great road journeys, a rough ribbon of tarmac and gravel climbing over passes above four thousand metres across the "roof of the world," past glacial lakes, yak herders, and the mountains of four countries.

properties.name: Gombe & Lake Tanganyika
properties.hook: The forested slopes above Lake Tanganyika where Jane Goodall began her famous study of wild chimpanzees, and where you can still hike into the trees to sit near our closest relatives, on the shore of one of the oldest and deepest lakes on earth.

properties.name: Mount Kilimanjaro
properties.hook: The highest mountain in Africa and the tallest free-standing mountain on earth, a snow-capped volcano you can walk to the top of, no ropes or ice axes needed, through five climate zones from rainforest to arctic summit.

properties.name: The Ngorongoro Crater
properties.hook: A vast, unbroken volcanic caldera whose sheer walls enclose a self-contained world of grassland, lake, and forest, packed with wildlife, a natural amphitheatre where you can see almost every big animal of the plains in a single day.

properties.name: The Southern Circuit
properties.hook: Tanzania's vast, wild, little-visited south, huge parks the size of small countries where you can watch wildlife along a great river with barely another vehicle in sight, the antidote to the crowded northern circuit.

properties.name: The Serengeti
properties.hook: The name means "endless plains," and that is exactly it: a sea of golden grassland stretching past the horizon, host to the largest overland animal migration on earth and to one of the greatest concentrations of predators anywhere.

properties.name: Zanzibar
properties.hook: A spice island in the Indian Ocean where an ancient Swahili trading town of carved doors and winding lanes gives way to turquoise water and white sand, a place of monsoon dhows, cloves, and a heavy history.

properties.name: The Andaman Coast
properties.hook: Thailand's turquoise southwest coast, where sheer limestone karsts rise straight out of warm clear water off white-sand beaches, some of them world-famous, over-loved, and worth escaping the crowds to enjoy.

properties.name: Bangkok
properties.hook: A hot, frenetic megacity where gold-spired temples and the royal Grand Palace sit amid a sprawl of markets, canals, sky trains, and some of the best street food on the planet.

properties.name: Chiang Mai & the North
properties.hook: The old capital of the northern Lanna kingdom, a moated old town packed with teak temples, ringed by forested mountains, hill villages, and ethical elephant sanctuaries, and far more relaxed than Bangkok.

properties.name: Koutammakou (the Batammariba)
properties.hook: A landscape of extraordinary fortress-like mud tower-houses, sculpted homes with turrets, granaries, and rooftop terraces, built by the Batammariba people, whose architecture and way of life are woven into their beliefs about the world.

properties.name: Lomé
properties.hook: A relaxed, palm-lined seaside capital on the Gulf of Guinea, best known for the Akodessewa fetish market, a startling open-air emporium of the ingredients of West African voodoo.

properties.name: El Djem
properties.hook: A gigantic Roman amphitheatre, one of the largest and best-preserved in the world, rising with astonishing completeness out of a small Tunisian town, a colosseum you can wander almost alone.

properties.name: Tunis, Carthage & Sidi Bou Said
properties.hook: The capital and its coast pack in three worlds: a labyrinthine medieval medina, the scattered ruins of Carthage, the great rival of Rome, and a blue-and-white clifftop village straight out of a painting.

properties.name: The Tunisian Sahara
properties.hook: Tunisia's slice of the Sahara: palm oasis towns, Berber granary-fortresses, a vast mirage-shimmering salt lake, and the desert sets where "Star Wars" filmed Luke Skywalker's home planet, Tatooine.

properties.name: Cappadocia
properties.hook: A surreal landscape of soft volcanic rock eroded into "fairy chimney" spires and honeycombed with cave homes, churches, and entire cities dug deep underground, all watched each dawn by a sky full of hot-air balloons.

properties.name: Ephesus
properties.hook: One of the best-preserved ancient cities in the Mediterranean, where you walk a real Roman main street of marble past temples, terraced houses, and the soaring two-storey façade of a great library.

properties.name: Göbekli Tepe
properties.hook: The oldest known monumental site on earth, carved T-shaped pillars raised around 11,600 years ago, older than Stonehenge by six thousand years and built before farming, pottery, writing, or the wheel.

properties.name: Istanbul
properties.hook: The only city that straddles two continents, capital of two great empires in turn, where a fifteen-hundred-year-old former cathedral, imperial mosques, and a labyrinthine bazaar crowd the hills above the Bosphorus.

properties.name: The Lycian Coast
properties.hook: A turquoise stretch of Mediterranean coast where pine-covered mountains drop into clear water, ancient Lycian tombs are carved into the cliffs, and a whole sunken city lies just under the surface off Kekova.

properties.name: Mount Nemrut
properties.hook: On a remote 2,100-metre summit, a little-known king built himself a tomb guarded by colossal seated statues of gods; earthquakes toppled their heads, which now sit on the ground staring out at the sunrise.

properties.name: Pamukkale & Hierapolis
properties.hook: A hillside of blinding white terraces built up over millennia by mineral-rich hot springs, where warm turquoise water pools on shelves of stone, topped by a Roman spa city and a pool you swim in among fallen marble columns.

properties.name: Ashgabat
properties.hook: A capital rebuilt almost entirely in gleaming white marble, with gold-domed ministries, giant monuments, and empty grand boulevards, one of the strangest and most tightly controlled cities on earth.

properties.name: The Darvaza Gas Crater
properties.hook: A wide pit in the Karakum desert that has been on fire for decades, a roaring crater of flame nicknamed the Door to Hell, at its most hypnotic when you camp beside it and watch it burn against the black desert night.

properties.name: Ancient Merv
properties.hook: The eroded remains of what was once among the largest cities in the world, a great Silk Road oasis reduced to walls, mounds, and a lone soaring mausoleum standing in the desert, after the Mongols destroyed it.

properties.name: Bwindi Impenetrable Forest
properties.hook: A dense, ancient rainforest cloaking steep mountains, home to roughly half the world's remaining mountain gorillas, where a hard trek through the tangled green rewards you with an hour beside a family of these gentle giants.

properties.name: Murchison Falls
properties.hook: Where the entire Nile is squeezed through a gap in the rocks just seven metres wide and explodes through in a violent, thundering torrent, at the heart of Uganda's largest national park.

properties.name: The Source of the Nile, Jinja
properties.hook: Where the world's longest river begins, the point at which the White Nile flows out of Lake Victoria to start its 6,000-kilometre journey to the Mediterranean, and one of Africa's best white-water rafting rivers.

properties.name: Kyiv
properties.hook: The ancient capital on the Dnipro, mother-city of the Slavic Orthodox world, its skyline gold with monastery domes, and since 2022 a city defending itself against full-scale invasion.

properties.name: Lviv
properties.hook: Ukraine's most Central European city, a Habsburg-era old town of cobbled squares, coffee houses, and layered Polish, Austrian, Jewish, and Ukrainian heritage, far from the front but still a country at war.

properties.name: The Ukrainian Carpathians
properties.hook: The green wooded mountains of Ukraine's far west, home to the Hutsul highlanders and their steep-roofed wooden churches, a world of forest, folk culture, and old ways in a country now at war.

properties.name: Abu Dhabi
properties.hook: The UAE's calmer, wealthier capital, home to one of the most beautiful modern mosques in the world and an outpost of the Louvre under a vast latticed dome that rains down "a shower of light."

properties.name: Dubai
properties.hook: A city that rose from a small pearling and trading port to a skyline of record-breaking towers in a single generation, headlined by the tallest building on earth, all air-conditioned excess on the edge of the desert.

properties.name: The Liwa Oasis
properties.hook: Where the UAE's malls and skylines give way to the real Arabia: a crescent of oasis villages at the foot of towering apricot dunes on the edge of the Empty Quarter, including one of the tallest dunes in the country.

properties.name: Eryri (Snowdonia)
properties.hook: The rugged mountain heart of Wales, crowned by Yr Wyddfa, the highest peak in England and Wales, with a Welsh-speaking culture and a slate-mining past written into the valleys below.

properties.name: The Giant's Causeway
properties.hook: Some forty thousand hexagonal basalt columns packed together at the sea's edge, so regular they look poured by hand. The old story says a giant built them as stepping stones to Scotland.

properties.name: Hadrian's Wall
properties.hook: The Roman Empire's northern edge, a stone wall run coast to coast across northern England around AD 122, still marching over the crags with forts and mile-castles strung along it.

properties.name: The Isle of Skye
properties.hook: A big Hebridean island of jagged black peaks and green landslip terraces, where the weather changes every twenty minutes and half the drama is watching it come across the water.

properties.name: The Lake District
properties.hook: A knot of green mountains and long lakes in England's northwest that invented a big part of how the modern world thinks about wild landscape, thanks to the Romantic poets who lived and walked here.

properties.name: Stonehenge & Avebury
properties.hook: The most famous prehistoric monument in the world, and a bigger, stranger stone circle twenty miles north that you can actually walk into with a pub in the middle of it.

properties.name: Bukhara
properties.hook: The most complete medieval city in Central Asia, a living Silk Road town of madrasas, minarets, and covered trading bazaars gathered around an old stone pool, that feels less restored museum than a place still lived in.

properties.name: Khiva
properties.hook: A walled desert city that looks almost too perfect to be real, a compact maze of mud-brick minarets, madrasas, and palaces enclosed by high ramparts, once the seat of a khanate and a notorious slave market.

properties.name: Samarkand
properties.hook: The great crossroads of the Silk Road, where the conqueror Timur built a capital of colossal turquoise-domed mosques and madrasas covered in dazzling blue tile, headlined by the three vast facing schools of the Registan.

properties.name: Ha Long Bay
properties.hook: Thousands of forested limestone towers rising straight out of a jade-green sea, an emerald maze of islets and caves that is one of Asia's iconic seascapes, and one of its most crowded.

properties.name: Hoi An
properties.hook: A beautifully preserved old trading port of ochre merchant houses and a Japanese covered bridge, strung at night with thousands of silk lanterns reflected in the river.

properties.name: The Mekong Delta
properties.hook: Where the Mekong finally splits into nine arms and empties into the sea, a green, watery world of rice paddies, orchards, and stilt houses, where whole markets float on boats.

properties.name: Old Sana'a
properties.hook: One of the oldest continuously inhabited cities on earth, a walled old town of tall gingerbread-like tower houses, their brown mud-brick façades iced with white geometric patterns, built up over more than a thousand years.

properties.name: Socotra
properties.hook: An isolated Indian Ocean island so evolutionarily strange it is called the Galápagos of the Arabian Sea, where umbrella-shaped "dragon's blood" trees bleed red sap and much of the plant life grows nowhere else on earth.

properties.name: Livingstone & Devil's Pool
properties.hook: The Zambian side of Victoria Falls, where in the low-water months you can swim to a natural rock pool right on the very lip of the cliff and peer over the edge as the Zambezi thunders down beside you.

properties.name: The Lower Zambezi
properties.hook: A stretch of the great Zambezi where the wildlife gathers along the river beneath an escarpment, and you drift past drinking elephants and yawning hippos in a canoe, at water level and in near silence.

properties.name: South Luangwa
properties.hook: The birthplace of the walking safari, a wild, uncrowded park along a meandering river where you explore on foot with an armed guide, and where night drives turn up some of the best leopard sightings in Africa.

properties.name: Great Zimbabwe
properties.hook: The largest ancient stone structure in sub-Saharan Africa, the ruined capital of a powerful medieval kingdom, its great curving walls built without mortar from millions of precisely-fitted granite blocks, and the monument that gave a nation its name.

properties.name: Hwange National Park
properties.hook: Zimbabwe's largest game reserve, a vast wilderness of teak forest and grassland famous for its enormous elephant herds, where in the dry season the wildlife gathers around waterholes kept alive by pumped water.

properties.name: Victoria Falls
properties.hook: The Zambezi crashing over a kilometre-and-a-half-wide cliff into a narrow gorge, throwing up a column of spray visible for miles, a curtain of falling water so vast that locals named it "the smoke that thunders."
